user_query:
I’m consolidating two observers’ gesture logs for a seminar on interpreting opponents’ body language, and I want our team’s notes aligned to a single shared coding scheme. Please do the following, working only within the local workspace:

1) Download the official 2017 COCO dataset person keypoints annotation JSON named “person_keypoints_val2017.json” from the cocodataset.org domain (the standard annotations set under “annotations”). Save the raw file to external/coco/person_keypoints_val2017.json. From its categories entry for “person”, extract the keypoints array and write those names to external/coco/keypoint_names.txt (one name per line, in the original order).

2) Inspect all CSV files under input/observations/. For each CSV, keep only rows where the cue value matches exactly one of the keypoint names you extracted. Save the filtered version of each file to outputs/standardized using the same basename with the suffix _standardized.csv (e.g., input/observations/obs_a.csv -> outputs/standardized/obs_a_standardized.csv). Preserve the original columns in the filtered outputs.

3) Create summaries from all matched rows across all input files combined:
   - By-session summary: For each session_id and keypoint, compute total_count (sum of count across observers/files) and percent_of_session = total_count divided by the sum of counts for all matched keypoints in that same session_id. Write this to outputs/summary/by_session.csv with columns: session_id,keypoint,total_count,percent_of_session.
   - Overall summary: Let total_sessions be the number of distinct session_id values found in the input files (regardless of whether a keypoint appears in a session). For each keypoint, compute total_count across all sessions and files, num_sessions = total_sessions, and mean_count_per_session = total_count / total_sessions. Write this to outputs/summary/overall.csv with columns: keypoint,total_count,num_sessions,mean_count_per_session.

4) Quality check for team synchronization: Aggregate all rows with cues that do not match the downloaded keypoint names. Sum their counts by (file_basename, cue) and write to outputs/qa/unmatched_cues.csv with columns: file,cue,total_count.

Inputs are in input/observations/. Required outputs are: external/coco/person_keypoints_val2017.json, external/coco/keypoint_names.txt, outputs/standardized/*.csv (one per input file), outputs/summary/by_session.csv, outputs/summary/overall.csv, and outputs/qa/unmatched_cues.csv. Please do not hardcode the keypoint list; derive it from the downloaded JSON. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
